perturbation.original_text: Qualigen hereby appoints Sekisui, and Sekisui accepts the appointment to act on an exclusive basis pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as a distributor for the sale of the Products in the Territory. Sekisui shall be permitted to appoint sub-distributors in the Territory (including any current Qualigen distributors) with the approval of Qualigen, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.changed_text: Qualigen hereby appoints Sekisui, and Sekisui accepts the appointment to act on an exclusive basis pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as a vendor for the sale of the Products in the Territory. Sekisui shall be permitted to appoint sub-distributors in the Territory (including any current Qualigen distributors) with the approval of Qualigen, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'distributor' has been changed to 'vendor' in Section 2.1. While Sekisui is appointed as a 'vendor,' the subsequent text refers to Sekisui appointing 'sub-distributors.' This creates an in-text contradiction, as it's unclear whether Sekisui's role as a 'vendor' allows them to appoint 'sub-distributors,' a term traditionally associated with distribution agreements. The agreement becomes ambiguous about Sekisui's authority and function.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Qualigen hereby appoints Sekisui, and Sekisui accepts the appointment to act on an exclusive basis pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, as a vendor for the sale of the Products in the Territory. Sekisui shall be permitted to appoint sub-distributors in the Territory (including any current Qualigen distributors) with the approval of Qualigen, not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Sekisui shall purchase the Products exclusively from Qualigen, and Qualigen shall supply the Products exclusively to Sekisui, in each case for the Territory. Qualigen shall assign to Sekisui Qualigen's agreements with Qualigen's current distributors (such that such current Qualigen distributors shall become Sekisui subdistributors), each of which is set forth on Schedule 2.1 hereto; if any of such agreements do not allow such assignment and the current distributor declines to consent to such an assignment to Sekisui, Qualigen shall (if Sekisui so requests) act pursuant to such agreement to terminate such agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'distributor' to 'vendor' directly contradicts the subsequent reference to 'sub-distributors' and the assignment of Qualigen's current distributor agreements to Sekisui, making them Sekisui's sub-distributors. This creates a clear and direct conflict because the role of a vendor does not typically include the appointment of sub-distributors or the management of a distribution network. The contradicted text explicitly states that current Qualigen distributors will become Sekisui's subdistributors, an action aligned with a distributor role but not clearly compatible with a vendor role, based on common business understanding. This creates ambiguity about Sekisui's ability to act as a distributor when the title given is a vendor.

perturbation.original_text: The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Products shall be established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Reagent Kits Products shall be based upon a formula intended to ensure that Sekisui will receive 90% of the total Available Margin for all Products during the first 12 months of this Agreement, 70% of the total Available Margin for all Products during months 13-24 of this Agreement, and 65% of the total Available Margin for all Products thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Products shall be established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Reagent Kits Products shall be based upon a formula intended to ensure that Sekisui will receive 90% of the total Net Profit for all Products during the first 12 months of this Agreement, 70% of the total Net Profit for all Products during months 13-24 of this Agreement, and 65% of the total Net Profit for all Products thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: The key term 'Available Margin' in section 4.1 is replaced with 'Net Profit'. 'Available Margin' has a specific definition in Exhibit A, while 'Net Profit' does not. This change creates an in-text contradiction because the contract specifies the calculation of the price based on a non-defined term. The actual calculation is defined for 'Available Margin', not 'Net Profit', making section 4.1 ambiguous and unenforceable as to the method of price setting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Products shall be established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits. The price that Sekisui shall pay for the Reagent Kits Products shall be based upon a formula intended to ensure that Sekisui will receive 90% of the total Net Profit for all Products during the first 12 months of this Agreement, 70% of the total Net Profit for all Products during months 13-24 of this Agreement, and 65% of the total Net Profit for all Products thereafter. Accordingly, the prices (established separately for the Reagent Kits and for the products other than Reagent Kits) to be paid by Sekisui for the Products shall be fixed (subject to a later lookback true up) for each respective prospective six month period in the manner set forth in Exhibit D. The initial prices Sekisui agrees to pay for the respective Products for the first such prospective "six month period" (in this instance actually a five month period: May-September 2016) shall be fixed (subject to a later lookback true up) in the manner set forth in Exhibit D. Thereafter such prices shall be revisited and recalculated (prospectively) every six months in the manner set forth in Exhibit D (i.e., for purposes of such calculations for establishing the new prospective prices for the reagent products, the applicable Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin shall be the Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin for the applicable historical 6-month period as defined in Exhibit D). In addition, on a semi-annual basis, such amount shall be reviewed based on the actual Net Revenue, COGS and Available Margin for the 6 months then ended. In the event that such review results in a difference from the intended share of Available Margin between the Parties as contemplated above, the Parties shall make a true up payment between them in order to compensate for such overpayment or shortfall, all as provided in Exhibit D. Any true-up payments shall be paid by the applicable Party within 30 days of the receipt of an invoice for the agreed to true up amount. Sekisui shall set the customer selling prices in good faith and in a commercially reasonable manner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Available Margin" with "Net Profit." The original text uses 'Available Margin,' which is defined in Exhibit A. The contradicted text also uses 'Net Profit.' However, the contradicted text then refers back to the calculation using 'Available Margin.' Since 'Net Profit' is not defined and the contradicted text still references the calculation using 'Available Margin', the change creates a strong in-text contradiction, particularly as it relates to the pricing mechanism described in Exhibit D. The contradicted text assumes 'Available Margin' is used to determine pricing and true-up payments, whereas the changed text now states that 'Net Profit' should be used, making the pricing mechanism ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The Business Plan sets forth Sekisui's plans for the sale and distribution of the Products, including target budget and resource allocations for the marketing and sales of the Products and estimated forecasts of sales to customers. Sekisui shall in good faith use commercially reasonable efforts, in conformance with good commercial practice and standards, government regulations and other applicable requirements, to promote, market and sell the Products, to execute the Business Plan and to achieve its objectives.
perturbation.changed_text: The Marketing Strategy sets forth Sekisui's plans for the sale and distribution of the Products, including target budget and resource allocations for the marketing and sales of the Products and estimated forecasts of sales to customers. Sekisui shall in good faith use commercially reasonable efforts, in conformance with good commercial practice and standards, government regulations and other applicable requirements, to promote, market and sell the Products, to execute the Business Plan and to achieve its objectives.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Business Plan' has been replaced with 'Marketing Strategy' in the first sentence of Section 4.4. However, the second sentence still refers to the 'Business Plan.' This creates an in-text contradiction because Sekisui is now expected to execute the 'Business Plan' even though the paragraph indicates Sekisui creates a 'Marketing Strategy'. The original text, creates an uncertanty to Sekisui on wich plan to execute, being the Business Plan the one in Exhibit B or the new created Marketing Strategy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Marketing Strategy sets forth Sekisui's plans for the sale and distribution of the Products, including target budget and resource allocations for the marketing and sales of the Products and estimated forecasts of sales to customers. Sekisui shall in good faith use commercially reasonable efforts, in conformance with good commercial practice and standards, government regulations and other applicable requirements, to promote, market and sell the Products, to execute the Business Plan and to achieve its objectives. Except as set forth in Section 3.2, such forecasts and budgets are intended for guidance purposes only and are not binding obligations. Sekisui shall be responsible for bad debt (customer nonpayment) and credit card merchant fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Business Plan' with 'Marketing Strategy' in the first sentence, while the second sentence still refers to the 'Business Plan'. The contradicted text, which is actually the same as the changed text, highlights this contradiction. This creates an in-text contradiction as Sekisui is directed to execute the 'Business Plan' while the paragraph now discusses a 'Marketing Strategy.' The definition of Misaligned Terminology applies, as the contract uses the terms 'Business Plan' and 'Marketing Strategy' in a confusing manner, leading to conflicting obligations and creating a strong, clear, and impactful contradiction.
